decks.name: 3D Shapes Around Us
decks.description: Learn about cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, and spheres that you see every day

cards.front: What is a cube? Give an example from daily life.
cards.back: A cube is a 3D shape where all 6 faces are equal squares. A dice (ludo dice), a Rubik's cube, and a sugar cube are all examples of cubes.
cards.hint: Think of a shape where every face looks exactly the same

cards.front: What is a cuboid? How is it different from a cube?
cards.back: A cuboid is a 3D shape with 6 rectangular faces. Unlike a cube, a cuboid's faces can be different sizes. A brick, a book, and a tiffin box are cuboids.
cards.hint: Think of a box shape where all sides are not equal

cards.front: What shape is a cricket ball or a marble?
cards.back: A cricket ball and a marble are shaped like a sphere. A sphere is perfectly round from every side. It has no edges, no corners, and just one curved surface.
cards.hint: Think of a perfectly round shape you can roll in any direction

cards.front: What shape does an ice cream cone have?
cards.back: An ice cream cone is shaped like a cone. A cone has a circular flat base at the bottom and a pointed tip at the top. A birthday cap and a funnel are also cone-shaped.
cards.hint: It has a round base and gets narrower to a point

cards.front: Name the shape of a pipe or a tin can.
cards.back: A pipe and a tin can are shaped like a cylinder. A cylinder has 2 circular flat faces (top and bottom) and 1 curved surface in the middle. A glass and a bangle are also cylinders.
cards.hint: It has circles on top and bottom connected by a curved surface

decks.name: Faces, Edges, and Vertices
decks.description: Understand the parts of 3D shapes - faces, edges, and vertices (corners)

cards.front: What is a face of a 3D shape?
cards.back: A face is a flat or curved surface of a 3D shape. A cube has 6 flat faces. A cylinder has 2 flat faces and 1 curved face. A sphere has only 1 curved face.
cards.hint: Touch any side of a box - that flat part is called a face

cards.front: What is an edge of a 3D shape?
cards.back: An edge is a line where two faces of a 3D shape meet. A cube has 12 edges. A cylinder has 2 curved edges. A sphere has 0 edges.
cards.hint: Run your finger along the side of a box - that line is an edge

cards.front: What is a vertex? How many vertices does a cube have?
cards.back: A vertex is a corner where edges meet. The plural is vertices. A cube has 8 vertices (4 on top, 4 on the bottom). A cone has 1 vertex (its pointed tip).
cards.hint: Think of the sharp corners of a box

cards.front: Which 3D shape has no edges and no vertices?
cards.back: A sphere has no edges and no vertices. It is perfectly round with only 1 curved surface. That is why a ball can roll easily in any direction!
cards.hint: Think of a ball - can you find any corners or sharp lines on it?

cards.front: How many faces, edges, and vertices does a cone have?
cards.back: A cone has 2 faces (1 flat circle + 1 curved surface), 1 curved edge (where the flat and curved faces meet), and 1 vertex (the pointed tip at the top).
cards.hint: Think of a birthday cap - it has a round bottom and a pointy top

decks.name: Angles and Shapes in Daily Life
decks.description: Explore angles in shapes and spot 3D shapes around you every day

cards.back: An angle is formed when two straight lines meet at a point. The corner of your notebook, the hands of a clock, and an open door all make angles. A square corner makes a right angle.
cards.hint: Look at the corner of your book - the two edges meeting form an angle

cards.front: What is a right angle? Where do you see it?
cards.back: A right angle is the angle at the corner of a square or rectangle. It looks like the letter L. The corners of your book, a door frame, and a window all have right angles.
cards.hint: Think of the shape of the letter L

cards.front: Name five 3D shapes you can find in your kitchen.
cards.back: In the kitchen you can find: a cylinder (glass or steel tumbler), a sphere (round ladoo or orange), a cuboid (tiffin box or matchbox), a cone (funnel), and a cube (sugar cube or paneer block).
cards.hint: Look at containers, fruits, and boxes in the kitchen

cards.front: Can a 3D shape have both flat and curved faces?
cards.back: Yes! A cylinder has 2 flat faces (circles) and 1 curved face. A cone has 1 flat face (circle) and 1 curved face. But a cube has only flat faces, and a sphere has only a curved face.
cards.hint: Think about a tin can - is every part flat?

cards.front: If you trace the bottom of a cylinder on paper, what shape do you get?
cards.back: You get a circle! When you trace any flat face of a 3D shape, you get a 2D shape. Tracing a cube's face gives a square. Tracing a cuboid's face gives a rectangle.
cards.hint: Put a glass upside down on paper and draw around it
